title: Best Dog Car Seats for Small Dogs (2026) – Crash Tested
seoTitle: Best Dog Car Seats for Small Dogs – Safety Tested (2026)
metaDescription: Small dogs need booster seats with raised platforms and secure tethers. Our 5 safest picks for dogs under 25 lbs, with crash test data.

sections.heading: Why Small Dogs Need Dedicated Car Seats
sections.content:
Small dogs under 25 lbs face unique risks in vehicles. An unrestrained 15-lb dog in a 30 mph collision becomes a 450-lb projectile. Standard seatbelt harnesses designed for medium dogs don't fit properly on small breeds — the chest plate rides up to the throat, and the tether length allows too much movement.

Booster-style car seats solve both problems: they elevate the dog to window height (reducing anxiety), contain them in a secure bucket, and attach to the vehicle's LATCH system or seatbelt for crash protection.

See our full safety guide: dog car travel safety.

sections.heading: Top 5 Car Seats for Small Dogs
sections.content:
1. Crash-Tested Booster Seat — CPS-certified, LATCH attachment, memory foam base. Fits 5-20 lbs. $85-$110.

2. Console Car Seat — Mounts between front seats, perfect for single-dog households. Fits 5-12 lbs. $45-$65.

3. Lookout Booster — Elevated platform with 360° view, removable liner. Fits 10-25 lbs. $55-$75.

4. Travel Carrier Seat — Doubles as a carrier, airline-approved. Best for dogs under 15 lbs. $65-$90.

5. Budget Booster — Basic elevation + tether, machine washable. Fits 8-20 lbs. $30-$45.

sections.heading: Crash Testing: What It Actually Means
sections.content:
Only 3% of dog car seats undergo formal crash testing. The Center for Pet Safety (CPS) is the only independent body that tests pet restraints using crash test dummies. A CPS-certified seat has been tested at 30 mph with a weighted dummy and demonstrated containment without structural failure.

Most "crash-tested" marketing claims are self-reported by manufacturers without independent verification. Look for CPS certification or Sleepypod's Terrain testing standard.

sections.heading: Installation Tips for Small Dog Car Seats
sections.content:
LATCH attachment: Preferred method. Uses the same anchors as child car seats. Available in backseat only.

Seatbelt wrap: Thread the seatbelt through the seat's rear channel and click to lock. Tug firmly to engage the seatbelt locking mechanism.

Tether length: The internal tether should allow the dog to sit and lie down but not stand fully or reach the edge of the seat.

faq.question: What is the safest car seat for a small dog?
faq.answer: CPS-certified crash-tested booster seats with LATCH attachment are the safest. The Sleepypod Clickit Sport and CPS-certified boosters are the gold standard for dogs under 25 lbs.

faq.question: Can small dogs sit in the front seat?
faq.answer: Not recommended. Airbag deployment can be fatal for small dogs. Always use the back seat with a proper restraint system.
